prompt: Pull the maximum-history price chart at daily fidelity for the YES token on 'Will Eric Trump win the 2028 US Presidential Election?' so I can see how the implied probability has tracked through the life of this contract and where the speculative spikes happened.
expectedReason: time-series chart needs enough history to show shape
responsePreview:
The price history for the **YES** token on Eric Trump winning the 2028 Presidential Election shows a market that has remained largely speculative, with implied probability fluctuating in a narrow band between **0.45% and 0.90%**.

As shown in the chart, the market experienced its most significant activity shortly after launch in late February 2026, followed by a gradual stabilization.

### Notable Price Moves & Speculative Spikes

| Date | Price (USD) | Implied Probability | Context / Move |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| 2026-02-20 | $0.0045 | 0.45% | Contract launch price |
| 2026-02-21 | $0.0075 | 0.75% | First major speculative spike |
| 2026-03-04 | $0.0090 | 0.90% | **All-time high** |
| 2026-03-20 | $0.0065 | 0.65% | Breakdown from the peak regime |
| 2026-03-22 | $0.0055 | 0.55% | Drop to a lower support level |
| 2026-04-09 | $0.0065 | 0.65% | Brief recovery and plateau |
| 2026-05-05 | $0.0055 | 0.55% | Current price (as of 08:28 UTC) |

### Market Summary
